title: uxget
html:
uxget

Copy field data from the I/O buffer to the specified program buffer.

int uxget (unsigned char*buffer, shortmax);

Parameters

buffer
Field data from the I/O buffer (Output)
max
Size of the buffer pointed to by buffer. This value should not be larger than the actual size of the buffer.

Return Values

Table 1. Values returned by uxget
Value Meaning
>0 The number of bytes copied into the buffer
0 The function was not called from the formatFromDbms trigger
<0 The negated number of bytes copied into the buffer. The negative status indicates that more data is available in the I/O buffer, and that you should call uxgetc to get the additional data.

Use

Library: yrtl

Description

uxget copies the contents of the I/O buffer to the specified program buffer. Because the formatFromDbms trigger is associated with a specific field, the I/O buffer contains the contents of that field, but be aware that this buffer is in DBMS storage format (that is, possibly dependent on packing code, database driver, and database driver settings).

If all field data was copied to the buffer, the function returns the number of bytes that it has copied. If there is more data to be copied, the function returns a negative value, and you should call uxgetc to obtain additional data, and repeat until uxgetc returns a positive status.

Profile and subfield separator characters, if present in a field, are also returned in the buffer, so you should be prepared to handle these. For information about the representation of these characters, see the "Subfield Separator and Profile Characters" topic.

This function can be called only by code performed or activated from the DECRYPT trigger. If it is called from another trigger, it will return zero, and the buffer will remain empty.

Example: uxget and uxgetc

The following example shows how to use uxget and uxgetc to obtain field data from the I/O buffer. Because the size of the field is assumed unknown, we read fixed chunks and expand the buffer as necessary. We use 100 as the size of the fixed chunks to read, although this is an arbitrary choice.

#include <h3gl.h>
#define CHUNKSIZE 100

EXPORT int getbuffer ()
{
char* buf;
int len;
int totlen;

len = uxget(buf, CHUNKSIZE); /* Get the first chunk of data */
totlen = abs(len); /* Set total length */

while ( len < 0 ) /* More to come */
{
buf = realloc(buf, totlen+CHUNKSIZE); /* Make room for next chunk*/
len = uxgetc(buf+totlen, CHUNKSIZE); /* Read next chunk */
totlen += abs(len); /* Update total length */
}

return totlen;
}
Related Links
  • Subfield Separator and Profile Characters

    Uniface uses part of the reserved area of each meta character set (that is, the first 32 characters plus the last character) to store characters with special meaning within Uniface, such as subfield separators and profile characters.

  • uxgetc

    Get additional field data from the I/O buffer.

  • uxput

    Copy field data from the specified program buffer to the I/O buffer.
